title: Add Telemetry Parser And Command Skeleton
description: Update `src/codex_orchestrator/cli.py` to register the `telemetry` subcommand, add its CLI flags, and wire dispatch to a new `command_telemetry(args, storage, console)` entry point that follows existing command signatures and patterns.
acceptance_criteria: `build_parser()` defines an `orchestrator telemetry` subparser with `--days`, `--feature-root`, `--agent-type`, `--status`, and `--json` options.
Main CLI dispatch routes the `telemetry` command to `command_telemetry()`.
The command entry point exists with the same service signature style as other CLI commands.

handoff_summary.completed: Registered `telemetry` subparser in `build_parser()` with all required flags. Implemented `command_telemetry()` entry point matching existing command signatures. Wired CLI dispatch in `main()`. Verified with syntax check and `--help`.
handoff_summary.remaining: Downstream bead B-715e8f90 may add real telemetry aggregation logic (runtime stats, token usage, latency histograms) behind this entry point.
handoff_summary.risks: The `--days` filter is parsed but not yet applied to execution history timestamps — a downstream bead implementing real telemetry queries will need to add that filtering logic.
handoff_summary.next_action: Downstream beads implementing telemetry aggregation can now call `command_telemetry` and extend it with real data queries.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts expected — only `cli.py` was modified and no sibling beads claim that file.

conflict_risks: No conflicts expected — only `cli.py` was modified and no sibling beads claim that file.

execution_history.summary: Added the `telemetry` subcommand to `cli.py`: registered the subparser with `--days`, `--feature-root`, `--agent-type`, `--status`, and `--json` flags; implemented `command_telemetry(args, storage, console)` as a skeleton that filters beads and emits JSON; wired dispatch in `main()`. Module imports cleanly and `orchestrator --help` confirms `telemetry` is listed.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
